title: cytisus supinus subsp. absinthioides
intro: Cytisus supinus subsp. absinthioides, also known as the Absinthe Broom or Woody Broom, is a lesser-known subspecies of the broom plant, a type of flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cytisus supinus subsp. absinthioides include relief from digestive discomforts such as bloating and cramps, as well as reduced inflammation in the body. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and availability may vary due to regulatory restrictions on broom plants in some regions.
